instructions.title: How to use this calculator
steps: Add founders by clicking 'Add Founder'
Adjust each person's time dedication using the slider
Enter the capital invested by each founder
Check if someone brought the idea or investor network
See the suggested split in the right panel
instructions.tip: The split is an initial suggestion. Negotiate based on each partner's actual contribution.

runway-calculator.title: Runway Calculator
runway-calculator.description: Discover how many months of capital you have

instructions.title: How to use this calculator
steps: Enter your current cash balance
Set the monthly burn rate (total expenses)
Add your current monthly revenue
Adjust the expected revenue growth rate
Analyze the 12-month projection chart
instructions.tip: Keep at least 6 months of runway to have time to pivot or raise funding.

instructions.title: How to use this generator
steps: Fill in company details (name, registration, address)
Add vesting beneficiary information
Configure vesting rules (periods and percentages)
Define protection and non-compete clauses
Download the PDF and review with a lawyer
instructions.tip: This is an initial template. Always consult a lawyer before signing.

instructions.title: How the quiz works
steps: Answer the 10 questions honestly
Choose the option that best represents you
At the end, discover your dominant archetype
See personalized tips for your profile
instructions.tip: There are no right or wrong answers. Be authentic!

unit-economics.title: Unit Economics Calculator
unit-economics.description: Calculate LTV, CAC and payback period

instructions.title: How to use this calculator
steps: Enter the average monthly ticket per customer
Define your product/service gross margin
Add the average customer lifetime (months)
Enter the customer acquisition cost (CAC)
Analyze results: LTV, payback and LTV/CAC ratio
instructions.tip: An LTV/CAC ratio above 3x is considered healthy for SaaS.

valuation-calculator.title: Valuation Calculator
valuation-calculator.description: Estimate your startup's value with DCF, comparables and Berkus

instructions.title: How to use this calculator
steps: Choose the valuation method (DCF, Multiples or Berkus)
Fill in the requested financial data
Compare different methods to get a value range
Use results as a basis for negotiations
instructions.tip: Investors use multiple methods. Be prepared to justify your valuation.

valuation.title: Valuation Calculator
valuation.description: Estimate your startup's value with DCF, comparables and Berkus

cap-table.title: Cap Table Simulator
cap-table.description: Model dilution and ownership across funding rounds

instructions.title: How to use this simulator
steps: Add founders and their initial ownership
Simulate funding rounds with valuation and capital
See how dilution affects each partner per round
Add employee option pool (ESOP)
Analyze the final cap table after all rounds
instructions.tip: Reserve 10-20% for ESOP before rounds to attract talent.

burn-rate-optimizer.title: Burn Rate Optimizer
burn-rate-optimizer.description: Identify cost-cutting opportunities and extend runway

instructions.title: How to use this optimizer
steps: Enter your expenses by category
Compare with market benchmarks
Identify categories above average
See personalized optimization suggestions
Calculate runway impact with savings
instructions.tip: Cut costs before you need to. It's easier to negotiate when you're not desperate.

burn-rate.title: Burn Rate Optimizer
burn-rate.description: Compare your expenses with market benchmarks and identify savings opportunities to extend your runway.

instructions.title: How to use this optimizer
steps: Select your startup's industry
Enter your monthly expenses by category
Compare with market benchmarks
Identify categories above average
Apply optimization suggestions
instructions.tip: Focus first on categories furthest from the benchmark. Small monthly savings have a big impact on runway.

instructions.title: How to use this canvas
steps: Start with the central Value Proposition
Define the Customer Segments you serve
Fill in Channels and Customer Relationships
List key Resources, Activities and Partners
Complete Cost Structure and Revenue Streams
instructions.tip: Validate each block by talking to real customers before building.

tam-sam-som.title: TAM SAM SOM Calculator
tam-sam-som.description: Calculate your market size using TAM, SAM and SOM methodology to present to investors

instructions.title: How to use this calculator
steps: Enter the TAM (total global market)
Define your geographic region %
Specify the % of segment you serve
Adjust realistic market share (SOM)
Compare with bottom-up calculation (ticket x customers)
instructions.tip: Investors prefer conservative and well-justified SOM over inflated TAM.

Map what they See and Hear in their environment

List the main Pains and Gains
instructions.tip: Use real customer interviews to fill in, not assumptions.

customer-dev.title: Customer Development Manual
customer-dev.description: Follow Steve Blank's process to validate your business before scaling

instructions.title: How to use this manual
steps: Follow the 4 phases in order: Discovery, Validation, Creation, and Building
Complete each phase's checklist before moving on
Use the key questions to guide your customer interviews
Mark completed tasks to track your progress
instructions.tip: Talk to at least 20 potential customers before validating any hypothesis. Real feedback is more valuable than online surveys.

intro.title: What is Customer Development?
intro.description: Customer Development is a methodology created by Steve Blank for startups to validate their business hypotheses through conversations with real customers before investing in scale.

discovery.title: Customer Discovery
discovery.subtitle: Validate the problem and segment
discovery.description: In this phase you test whether the problem you want to solve actually exists and if customers care enough to pay for a solution.

questions.q1: Is the problem you identified real and frequent?
questions.q3: How much would they pay to solve this problem?

tasks.task1: Define hypotheses about customer, problem and solution
tasks.task2: Create interview script (don't mention your solution)
tasks.task4: Document patterns and insights from conversations
tasks.task5: Refine personas and segments based on data

discovery.tip: In interviews, focus on understanding the customer's past behavior, not what they would hypothetically do. Ask about the last time they faced the problem.

validation.title: Customer Validation
validation.subtitle: Test the sales model
validation.description: Now you test whether you can sell your solution in a repeatable and scalable way, validating channels and messages.

questions.q1: Does your value proposition resonate with customers?
questions.q2: Can you convert leads into sales?

tasks.task1: Create MVP or prototype to test
tasks.task3: Sell to early adopters (minimum 10 paying customers)

validation.tip: Don't optimize prematurely. If you can't sell 'artisanally', scaling won't solve the problem.

creation.title: Customer Creation
creation.subtitle: Scale demand
creation.description: With a validated model, it's time to scale customer acquisition through structured marketing and sales.

questions.q2: What is the CAC and LTV of your customers?
questions.q3: How to accelerate growth while maintaining quality?

tasks.task1: Define go-to-market strategy

creation.tip: Focus on 2-3 channels that work before diversifying. Depth > breadth at this stage.

building.title: Company Building
building.subtitle: Transition to scale
building.description: Transform the startup into a company with processes, culture and structure for sustainable growth.

questions.q1: Can the company operate without the founders day-to-day?
questions.q3: Is the culture defined and being preserved?

tasks.task1: Structure departments and leadership

building.tip: Hire people better than you in areas you don't master. Your role now is to build the team, not do everything.

resources.subtitle: Essential books to deepen your knowledge

lgpd-guide.title: LGPD Guide for SaaS
lgpd-guide.description: Complete guide to comply with Brazil's General Data Protection Law

instructions.title: How to use this manual
steps: Follow the 4 phases: Basics, Collection, Security, and Rights
Complete each checklist to ensure compliance
Read the key points to understand legal requirements
Track your overall progress at the top of the page
instructions.tip: Document all compliance actions. In case of audits, having organized records demonstrates good faith and may reduce penalties.

intro.title: What is LGPD?
intro.description: The General Data Protection Law (Law 13.709/2018) establishes rules about collection, storage, processing and sharing of personal data. For SaaS, compliance is essential to avoid fines and build customer trust.

warning.title: Legal Notice
warning.description: This guide is informational only and does not replace specialized legal advice. Consult a lawyer for specific compliance for your company.

basics.title: LGPD Fundamentals
basics.subtitle: Essential concepts to get started
basics.description: Understand the basic principles of the law and how it applies to your SaaS business.

points.p1: Personal data is any information that identifies a person (name, email, ID, IP, etc)
points.p2: You need a legal basis to process data (consent, contract, legitimate interest, etc)
points.p3: Data subjects have rights over their data (access, correction, deletion, portability)

tasks.task1: Map all personal data your application collects
tasks.task2: Identify the purpose of each collected data
tasks.task3: Define the legal basis for each processing
tasks.task5: Create communication channel for data subjects

basics.tip: Start with data mapping. You cannot protect what you don't know. List all personal information in a spreadsheet.

collection.title: Data Collection
collection.subtitle: Best practices for capture
collection.description: Implement data collection transparently and with adequate consent.

points.p2: Clearly inform what the data will be used for
points.p3: Obtain specific and granular consent when needed

tasks.task1: Create clear and accessible privacy policy

collection.tip: Use separate checkboxes for different purposes (marketing, analytics, sharing). Never use pre-checked boxes.

security.title: Data Security
security.subtitle: Technical and organizational protection
security.description: Implement security measures to protect data against leaks and unauthorized access.

points.p1: Encryption is mandatory for sensitive data in transit and at rest
points.p3: Audit logs allow tracking access and changes

tasks.task1: Implement HTTPS throughout application

security.tip: Document all security measures implemented. In case of inspection, you'll need to demonstrate compliance.

rights.title: Data Subject Rights
rights.subtitle: Handling requests
rights.description: Prepare your application to handle data subject requests about their personal data.

points.p1: Subjects can request access, correction or deletion of their data
points.p2: You have 15 days to respond to simple requests
points.p3: Portability allows transferring data to another service

tasks.task1: Create page/form for data subject requests
tasks.task2: Implement data export in readable format

rights.tip: Automate as much as possible. An 'Export my data' button in the user profile reduces operational load.

penalties.title: Penalties for Non-Compliance
penalties.subtitle: Fines and sanctions provided by law
penalties.p1: Warning with deadline for corrective measures
penalties.p2: Simple fine of up to 2% of revenue (limited to R$50 million per infraction)
penalties.p4: Suspension or prohibition of data processing

intro.title: Why is the Founding Team Crucial?

mvp-vibecoding.title: MVP with Vibecoding Guide
mvp-vibecoding.description: Complete guide to build your MVP using AI tools like Lovable, Cursor and v0

instructions.title: How to use this manual
steps: Follow the 4 phases in order: Fundamentals, Preparation, Building, and Launch
Complete each phase's checklist before moving on
Use the key points to guide your product decisions
Avoid common red flags that delay MVPs
instructions.tip: Focus on validating one hypothesis at a time. A perfect MVP that takes 6 months is worse than an imperfect MVP launched in 2 weeks.

intro.title: What is Vibecoding?
intro.description: Vibecoding is the practice of building software using AI tools that transform descriptions into functional code. With well-written prompts, you can create a complete MVP in days, not months.

warning.title: Important
warning.description: Vibecoding accelerates execution, but doesn't replace strategic thinking. Understand your problem and user before you start building.

fundamentals.title: Fundamentals
fundamentals.subtitle: Understand the vibecoding ecosystem
fundamentals.description: Learn about the main tools available and when to use each one to maximize your productivity.

points.p1: Lovable: ideal for complete web apps with integrated backend
points.p2: Cursor/v0: better for projects requiring technical customization
points.p3: Bolt/Replit: great for quick prototypes and experiments

tasks.task1: Define product type (web app, landing page, internal tool)
tasks.task2: Choose main tool based on project
tasks.task3: Create account and explore basic interface

fundamentals.tip: Start with a small project to understand how the tool interprets your prompts before starting the real MVP.

preparation.title: Preparation
preparation.subtitle: Define scope before coding
preparation.description: A simplified PRD and well-defined scope save hours of rework and avoid feature creep.

points.p1: Write a 1-page PRD with problem, solution and core features
points.p2: Separate features into Must-have vs Nice-to-have
points.p3: Define measurable success criteria for the MVP

tasks.task1: Write simplified PRD (1 page)

preparation.tip: Your MVP should solve ONE problem very well. If you need more than 5 core features, it's too complex.

building.title: Building
building.subtitle: Effective prompting techniques
building.description: Your MVP quality depends on your prompt quality. Be specific, iterative and test frequently.

points.p1: Break large tasks into smaller, specific prompts
points.p2: Include context: 'I'm building a [type] for [user] that [problem]'

tasks.task1: Create basic app structure (layout, navigation)
tasks.task2: Implement core features one at a time
tasks.task4: Test main flows as a real user
tasks.task5: Fix bugs and improve UX based on testing

building.tip: When something doesn't work, explain the problem in detail in the next prompt. Include errors and expected vs actual behavior.

launch.title: Launch
launch.subtitle: From code to real users
launch.description: An MVP only has value when it's in real users' hands. Launch fast, collect feedback, iterate.

points.p1: Set up custom domain for credibility

tasks.task1: Deploy to production

launch.tip: Don't wait to be perfect to launch. If you're not embarrassed by v1, you took too long to launch.

redFlags.title: Common Pitfalls
redFlags.subtitle: Mistakes that delay your MVP
redFlags.f1: Over-engineering: adding features users didn't ask for
redFlags.f2: Feature creep: expanding scope with each iteration
redFlags.f3: Not testing: building for weeks without real user feedback
redFlags.f4: Vague prompts: generic instructions generate generic code and bugs

contract.totalEquityHint: Total percentage to be granted to the beneficiary over the vesting period

pdf.introText: By this private instrument, on one side:
pdf.agreementIntro: The parties, by mutual agreement, resolve to enter into this Vesting Contract, which shall be governed by the following clauses and conditions:
pdf.clause1Title: CLAUSE ONE - OBJECT OF THE CONTRACT
pdf.clause1Content: This contract aims to grant equity participation rights to the BENEFICIARY, in a graduated manner, according to the conditions and terms established in this instrument, linked to the fulfillment of goals or permanence in the COMPANY.
pdf.beneficiaryWillHave: The BENEFICIARY shall be entitled to up to
pdf.equityParticipation: of equity participation in the COMPANY, acquired gradually according to the schedule below
pdf.defaultConditions: The acquisition of shares is subject to full compliance with the goals or conditions described in Annex I of this contract.
pdf.lossOfRightsContent: If the BENEFICIARY fails to comply with the established conditions or terminates their relationship with the COMPANY before the expected period, they will lose the right to shares not acquired by the date of termination.
pdf.clause3Title: CLAUSE THREE - PROTECTION OF ORIGINAL OWNERS
pdf.controlPreservationContent: Regardless of the acquisition of shares by the BENEFICIARY, the original owner-partners of the COMPANY must maintain at least {{percentage}}% of shareholding control and the exclusive right of management and strategic command of the COMPANY, unless expressly provided in a subsequent agreement signed by all parties.
pdf.votingRestrictionsContent: The BENEFICIARY, upon acquiring equity shares, agrees to exercise voting rights in accordance with shareholder agreements and strategic guidelines defined by the original owner-partners.
pdf.nonCompeteContent: The BENEFICIARY agrees, during the term of this contract and for {{years}} year(s) after the termination of the relationship, not to engage in activities competing with those of the COMPANY.
pdf.confidentialityContent: All confidential information obtained during the relationship with the COMPANY must be kept confidential, under penalty of civil and criminal liability.
pdf.terminationIntro: This contract shall be considered terminated by operation of law in the following cases
pdf.terminationReason1: Non-compliance with any of the clauses by the BENEFICIARY
pdf.terminationReason2: Termination of the BENEFICIARY's relationship with the COMPANY
pdf.terminationConsequence: In case of termination, the BENEFICIARY will immediately lose the right to shares not acquired.
pdf.irrevocable: This contract is entered into irrevocably and irreversibly, binding the parties and their successors to comply with it in all its terms.
pdf.jurisdictionContent: The court of {{city}} is hereby elected, waiving any other, however privileged, to resolve any disputes arising from this contract.
pdf.closingText: And being thus fair and agreed, the parties sign this instrument in {{copies}} copies of equal content and form, in the presence of {{witnesses}} witnesses.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about co-founding.
faq.description: Everything you need to know before finding your co-founder on Guilda.
faq.q1: What is a Builder and a Seller on Guilda?
faq.a1: A Builder is someone who builds the product (developer, engineer, designer, PM). A Seller handles the business (sales, marketing, growth hacking). Guilda connects these two complementary profiles to form complete co-founder teams for startups.
faq.q2: How does Social Payment work to get Founder access?
faq.a2: It's our alternative access method: you publish a post about Guilda on LinkedIn, Instagram or other networks, submit the link for AI validation and, if approved, get 6 months of Founder plan for free.
faq.q3: How long does it take to find a co-founder on Guilda?
faq.a3: It depends on your profile, but our match rate is 94%. Many founders find complementary partners in less than a week. The algorithm analyzes skill and goal compatibility to suggest the ideal partner.
faq.q4: Do I need a ready idea to use the platform?
faq.a4: Not necessarily. Many matches come from complementary skill combinations between Builders and Sellers. Developers can find projects to earn equity. But if you already have an idea or MVP, the validation process is even faster.
faq.a5: No. Guilda is the co-founder community for any professional who wants to co-found a startup — Builders (tech/product), Sellers (business/growth), Starters (beginners with potential) and even Investors looking for early-stage startups.

meta.title: Privacy Policy | Guilda
meta.description: Learn how Guilda collects, uses, and protects your personal data.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Guilda is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your personal information when you use our platform.

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.content:
We collect the following information when you use Guilda:

• Account information: name, email, profile picture
• Profile data: bio, skills, archetype (Builder/Seller)
• Usage data: platform interactions, matches, messages
• Technical data: IP address, browser type, device

When you log in with Google, we receive basic information from your public profile.

purpose.title: Purpose of Processing
purpose.content:
We use your data to:

• Create and manage your account
• Connect you with compatible co-founders
• Improve our matching algorithms
• Send notifications about matches and messages
• Prevent fraud and ensure platform security
• Comply with legal obligations

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis
legalBasis.content:
The processing of your data is based on:

• Consent: by creating your account, you consent to data processing
• Contract performance: necessary to provide our services
• Legitimate interest: to improve the platform and prevent fraud
• Legal compliance: when required by law

As provided by applicable data protection laws.

thirdParties.title: Sharing with Third Parties
thirdParties.content:
We may share your data with:

• Google: for OAuth authentication
• Supabase: our infrastructure and database provider
• Resend: for sending transactional emails
• Authorities: when required by law

We do not sell your personal data to third parties.

userRights.title: Your Rights
userRights.content:
You have the right to:

• Confirm the existence of processing
• Access your data
• Correct incomplete or outdated data
• Anonymize, block, or delete unnecessary data
• Data portability
• Delete data processed with consent
• Revoke consent

To exercise your rights, contact us at: contato@guilda.app.br

cookies.title: Cookies and Similar Technologies
cookies.content:
We use essential cookies to:

• Keep your session active
• Remember your language and theme preferences
• Ensure platform security

We do not use third-party tracking or advertising cookies.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.content:
We keep your data while your account is active.

After account deletion:
• Profile data is removed immediately
• Message data is anonymized
• Technical logs are kept for up to 6 months
• Data required for legal compliance is kept as required

contact.title: Contact
contact.content:
For privacy questions:

• Email: contato@guilda.app.br
• Location: São Paulo, Brazil

Data Protection Officer (DPO): Guilda
Email: contato@guilda.app.br

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms

service.title: Service Description

Our services include:

eligibility.title: Eligibility
eligibility.content:
To use Guilda, you must:

• Be at least 18 years old
• Provide true and accurate information
• Not have been previously banned from the platform
• Have full legal capacity

Use by minors under 18 is expressly prohibited.

account.title: Your Account
account.content:
You are responsible for:

• Maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials
• All activities performed on your account
• Immediately notifying any unauthorized use

Do not share your password. Each account is individual and non-transferable.

conduct.title: Prohibited Conduct
conduct.content:
It is prohibited to:

• Provide false or misleading information
• Harass, threaten, or intimidate other users
• Send spam or unsolicited messages
• Use the platform for illegal activities
• Attempt to access other users' accounts
• Scrape or automatically collect data
• Create multiple accounts
• Sell or transfer your account

Violations may result in suspension or permanent ban.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual.content:
Guilda and its content are protected by intellectual property laws.

You retain rights to content you create (projects, bio, etc.).

By publishing content, you grant us a license to display it on the platform.

Do not use Guilda's brands, logos, or content without express authorization.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content:
Guilda is provided "as is".

We do not guarantee:
• That the service will be uninterrupted or error-free
• That you will find a co-founder
• The success of any partnership formed

We are not responsible for:
• Actions or omissions of other users
• Indirect or consequential damages
• Loss of data or lost profits

modifications.title: Modifications
modifications.content:
We may modify these terms at any time.

We will notify significant changes by email or on the platform.

Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

If you do not agree with the changes, you must stop using the platform.

jurisdiction.title: Applicable Law and Jurisdiction
jurisdiction.content:
These terms are governed by the laws of Brazil.

The courts of São Paulo/SP have jurisdiction to resolve any disputes.

Before any legal action, parties must attempt to resolve disputes amicably.

q1.question: What is the Starter archetype?
q1.answer: A Starter is someone with potential and ambition, but still at the beginning of their entrepreneurial journey. You may not have proven technical or commercial experience, but you have the desire to learn and contribute. It's the starting point for those who want to enter the startup world without pressure.

q2.question: What can I do on the platform as a Starter?
q2.answer:
• Explore the Tavern to meet Builders, Sellers and Investors
• Access the Library with essential books for founders
• Read blog articles about validation, customer development and growth
• Participate in Missions to earn XP and level up
• Apply for startup positions as an apprentice or contributor

q3.question: How to evolve from Starter to Builder or Seller?
q3.answer: As you develop your skills, you can update your profile to Builder (if you focused on product/code) or Seller (if you focused on sales/growth). Go to "Edit Profile" and change your archetype when you feel ready. There's no deadline - evolve at your own pace.

q4.question: Can I participate in the Acceleration Program?
q4.answer: The 15-day program is for Builder+Seller pairs with an idea to validate. As a Starter, you can participate if you find an experienced partner willing to mentor you during the program. Use the Tavern to find founders open to teaching.

q5.question: How to find a mentor or first opportunity?
q5.answer: Use the Tavern to filter for more experienced profiles. Send connection invites explaining that you're a Starter and looking to learn. Many founders value motivated people willing to contribute even without formal experience.

q6.question: What resources to use for learning?
q6.answer:
• **Library**: Books like "Lean Startup", "Business Model Generation"
• **Blog**: Articles on validation, first sale, customer discovery
• **Missions**: Practical tasks that teach concepts while you execute
• **FAQ**: This guide you're reading now!

q1.question: How to build credibility without experience?
q1.answer: Show proactivity. Complete your profile with a sincere bio about your goals. Participate in discussions, help other users. Credibility comes from consistency, not a resume.

q2.question: How to use the Tavern for initial networking?
q2.answer: Filter by archetypes that interest you. Read bios before connecting. Send personalized messages explaining why you want to connect and what you want to learn. Be specific, not generic.

q3.question: Which jobs are ideal for Starters?
q3.answer: Look for positions that mention "apprentice", "internship", "contributor" or "low equity/high learning". Startups in ideation or MVP phase are more open to Starters than companies in traction.

q4.question: When to migrate from Starter to Builder/Seller?
q4.answer: When you have: 1) A personal project in your portfolio, 2) Demonstrable skills, 3) Practical experience (even if small). It doesn't need to be perfect - the migration is a milestone, not a diploma.

q5.question: How to use the Library to accelerate learning?
q5.answer: Start with "Lean Startup" to understand validation. Then "Business Model Generation" for business modeling. Read 1 chapter per day and immediately apply in Mission exercises.

autoIntro.question: What are auto-introductions?
autoIntro.answer: The system analyzes complementary profiles and suggests connections automatically. If you're a Builder looking for a Seller, you can receive introductions from Sellers with skills that complement yours. Accept or ignore the suggestions in your notification feed.

commander.question: How does the Commander help me?
commander.answer: During Acceleration, the Commander evaluates your deliverables and recommends specific resources: articles about sales on the blog, pricing books in the Library, or connections in the Tavern when you need complementary skills.
